Container.Name: ai-tg-bot
Container.Overview: Pi-powered Telegram agent using a separately operated local OpenSandbox server for persistent per-user command environments.
Container.Requires: Telegram, OpenRouter, Tavily, and OpenSandbox API keys; a reachable OpenSandbox server; and one host folder mounted by both containers. Codex OAuth and Docling are optional.

Config.Name: Appdata
Config.Description: Persistent SQLite database, Pi sessions/OAuth credentials, and legacy workspace data retained for migration.

Config.Name: Agent Shared Data
Config.Description: Persistent user workspaces and canonical managed chat files. Use this exact host path for OpenSandbox Shared Host Root and the server storage mount.

Config.Name: Telegram Bot Token
Config.Description: BotFather token.

Config.Name: OpenRouter API Key
Config.Description: OpenRouter key for fallback inference, image generation, and embeddings.

Config.Name: Tavily API Key
Config.Description: Tavily key for web search and extraction.

Config.Name: OpenSandbox API Key
Config.Description: Must match OPENSANDBOX_SERVER_API_KEY on the OpenSandbox server.

Config.Name: OpenSandbox Domain
Config.Description: OpenSandbox lifecycle server host and port. Put both containers on the same custom Docker network when using this container name.

Config.Name: OpenSandbox Protocol
Config.Description: Use http on the private Docker network or https when a trusted reverse proxy terminates TLS.

Config.Name: OpenSandbox Shared Host Root
Config.Description: Unraid host path selected for Agent Shared Data. OpenSandbox passes this path to the host Docker daemon, so it must match exactly.

Config.Name: OpenSandbox Image
Config.Description: Ubuntu agent runner image. Pin a sha-* tag in production.

Config.Name: OpenSandbox User
Config.Description: Username used to own private stdin files inside the runner. It must exist in the image and resolve to OpenSandbox UID.

Config.Name: OpenSandbox Group
Config.Description: Group name used to own private stdin files inside the runner. It must exist in the image and resolve to OpenSandbox GID.

Config.Name: OpenSandbox UID
Config.Description: Nonzero numeric UID used for commands inside the sandbox; keep it aligned with APP_UID for shared-file exports.

Config.Name: OpenSandbox GID
Config.Description: Nonzero numeric GID used for commands inside the sandbox.

Config.Name: Application UID
Config.Description: Non-root UID used by the bot. Keep aligned with the runner image and shared-folder ownership.

Config.Name: Application GID
Config.Description: Primary non-root GID used by the bot.

Config.Name: Database URL
Config.Description: SQLite or PostgreSQL database URL.

Config.Name: Pi Agent Directory
Config.Description: Persistent Pi configuration, OAuth credentials, and thread sessions.

Config.Name: Legacy Bash Root
Config.Description: Pre-sandbox workspace source retained for migration and rollback.

Config.Name: Agent Shared Root
Config.Description: Bot-container view of the shared filesystem.

Config.Name: Managed File Root
Config.Description: Canonical chat-file store outside per-user sandbox mounts.

Config.Name: Docling URL
Config.Description: Optional external Docling server.

Config.Name: OpenSandbox Idle Pause
Config.Description: Pause a user's sandbox after this many idle milliseconds.

Config.Name: OpenSandbox Ready Timeout
Config.Description: Maximum create/resume readiness time in milliseconds.

Config.Name: Bash Timeout
Config.Description: Maximum milliseconds for one sandbox command.

Config.Name: Bash Max Output
Config.Description: Maximum characters returned for each stdout or stderr stream.

Config.Name: Pi Turn Timeout
Config.Description: Maximum milliseconds for a Pi turn.

Config.Name: Log Level
Config.Description: debug, info, warn, or error.
